task_completion.description: Verify that the original trapezoidal fins were removed and a tube fin set was added to the rocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree (top left), 'Trapezoidal fin set' should be missing, and 'Tube fin set' should be present and attached to the body tube. The 2D/3D rocket view should show tube fins.

task_completion.description: Verify the tube fins are configured with the correct dimensions and count.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Tube Fin configuration dialog in OpenRocket. It should show: Number of fins = 6, Length = 70 mm, Outer diameter = 20 mm (or Outer radius = 10 mm), and Thickness = 1.0 mm.

task_completion.description: Verify that a flight simulation was run for the new tube-fin configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket 'Flight simulations' tab, there should be a completed simulation row showing calculated metrics like Apogee, Max velocity, etc., indicating the simulation was successfully executed.

task_completion.description: Verify the modified rocket design was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title bar should display 'tube_fin_conversion.ork', or a file manager/terminal screenshot should show 'tube_fin_conversion.ork' located in '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/'.

task_completion.description: Verify the comparative performance report was written with the required content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of 'tube_fin_report.md' (or .txt) in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/'. The text must be visibly substantial (at least ~200 words), explicitly mention both 'trapezoidal' and 'tube' fins, and include specific numerical values for apogee, velocity, and stability margin.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenRocket graphical interface being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands or text editors modifying the .ork XML directly.

integrity.description: Verify the report contains genuine simulation data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The performance metrics (apogee, velocity, stability margin) in the report should be specific numbers that plausibly come from the OpenRocket simulation tab, not generic placeholders like '[Insert Apogee Here]'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent wrote an original comparative report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report text should be a coherent analysis comparing the two fin types, not just a copy-paste of the task description instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target rocket file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/tube_fin_conversion.ork'. The report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/tube_fin_report.md' (or .txt). The tube fins must have exactly 6 fins, 70 mm length, 20 mm outer diameter (10 mm radius), and 1.0 mm thickness. The report must be at least 200 words and explicitly mention both fin types (trapezoidal and tube) along with apogee, velocity, and stability margin.
